title: Fish and Chips
content:
I have found over the years that people really cannot agree on what constitutes good fish and chips. Everyone has different tastes — I prefer a crispier batter and don’t like a pool of grease. So don’t waste your time listening to other fukwits, find your own way.

We tried Audreys in Bridlington under duress from the mother-in-law. She had said a few times she had been without us and it was fantastic.

My wife agreed they were absolutely fucking shit. They were the 3rd most expensive we had ever sat down to eat — the fish didn’t taste like fish and it was strangely flat, no flakes of meat and small yellow grease.

At the end of the meal the mother-in-law said, "well that was absolutely gorgeous," to our complete dismay. Unsure if she said it because she recommended it, because she likes that it’s named after a woman, or just that she really does like them.

We tried Busy Bees in Bridlington with her as she said she enjoyed them. They couldn’t be more different to Audreys — different batter, dark not yellow, thicker fish and no grease.

So who knows — and they were 5 quid less.

Eat where you want and tell others to fuck right off.

Same thing with curries too.

Daft-a-peth

title: Playing Golf While Legs Are Fucked
content:
It’s been somewhat of a mental struggle at times — playing a sport where 35-year-old you would be beaten by the 13-year-old version of you.

But all the shit in between those two ages meant the chance of me playing again could have been slim.

Now 38-year-old me gets out generally once a week for 6 months of the year, and I’ve gotten it out of my head that once I was good and just enjoy playing…

Well, that was until I posted 6 rounds on the spin of 80–84. I generally feel like I could be single figures still (currently 17.5).

Then came the bad round — a stinking 90. Couldn’t keep the ball in play off the tee, and it’s a downturn in form that tends to occur when Royal Ogden dries out. It doesn’t suit my game, and this year I’m going to do something about it.

If I can rebuild my golf swing to take pressure off my joints, surely I can do something that helps with the conditions.

I think I need a lower flight from both tee and fairway — something I am shit at.

So when I’m playing alone in the mornings after staying up for the World Cup, I plan to try improve that skill.

Daft-a-peth

title: Betting Yarn
content:
A rather large US betting operator, in conjunction with CBS, offered some boosted prices on Birmingham and Wrexham’s antepost offerings.

They had league winners, promotion, top 6, top half — all at higher than the rest of the industry.

They had gotten themselves into a bit of a pickle because:

1. They had nowhere to lay the action off
2. They had taken a phenomenal amount of money on these two
3. The two teams alone made 98% of their antepost book
4. They have no fucking clue how to run a proper bookmaker

So they called us.

I received an email from the bossman outlining the numbers and general terms. It’s our first year doing the Championship and I didn’t want any fuck-ups.

But in any other league we’d have said YES in less than 5 seconds. Very easy dollar to be made.

So we underwrote the bets, hoping for a nice free slice by May.

Bottom line was:
One getting promoted by winning the league and another getting up by playoffs or second would fucking kill you.

But the fact they were both 11/1 to win the league was appetising to a fact merchant.

Two high-budget teams going from League 1 and both getting up would be some turn of events.

I didn’t rate either manager to be honest. I especially thought Parkinson may have found his level.

At the time, the transfer window was still open, so we could be caught out.

My decision was to stand all positions and trade out later.

The bossman gave me the old "are you sure?" line — and I was.

I was sure I could trade out, and sure two wouldn’t go up.

With Ipswich, Southampton, Leicester, Coventry, Boro in the mix — I felt I had enough bullets.

(Leicester had shit out and we backed them for relegation by October. Southampton were playing great, couldn’t score, changed manager and were bottom 3 — LOL)

By September I thought Blues had better players but worse team.

Wrexham looked solid — proper Championship-type side.

Money kept flowing in for both teams despite poor starts.

By December, Birmingham were 9th. I watched a few games and thought their home form would turn.

I stood them both.

Pre-Xmas is where everything swings — loads of games, table moves fast.

I nearly backed Wrexham promotion to reduce liability… left it.

They lost to Swansea — decision justified.

Then Boxing Day chaos:
3–0 comeback vs Sheff Utd, then 3 wins.

We had to act — laid them at 10s (they were 20s before Xmas).

Missed best price, but that’s the game.

They drifted → shortened → drifted again.

We traded around it and made them a winner for us.

By the end:
If customers had just let it run, they’d have cleaned up.

Instead, we clipped 30% and used their exposure to trade all season.

Strange industry.
